name: Sierra de El Cañavate Loop from Vara de Rey
name_original: Sierra de El Cañavate – circular desde Vara del Rey por Cañada Juncosa
description: Mountain biking the Sierra de El Cañavate circular route from Vara del Rey offers a moderate 26.0-mile ride with 1205 feet of gain.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vara De Rey offers routes through varied terrain, including agricultural plains and areas with karst depressions. The region features gentle rolling hills and some more elevated sections, particularly around the Sierra de El Cañavate. These landscapes provide a mix of open paths and trails that traverse rural settings. The routes are generally well-suited for exploring the local environment on two wheels. Best mountain bike trails around Vara De Rey The most popular mountain bike trail is Las Torcas de Sisante Loop, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 37 minutes to complete, navigating through distinctive geological formations. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de El Cañavate Loop from Vara de Rey, a moderate 26.0 miles (41.8 km) path. This route features ascents into the Sierra de El Cañavate, offering elevated views. Local mountain bikers also love the Sisante loop from Sisante, a 25.5 miles (41.1 km) trail leading through rural landscapes and agricultural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Mountain biking around Vara De Rey is defined by agricultural plains, gentle hills, and occasional karst features, with routes suitable for various ability levels. The network includes options for both moderate and easier rides. The routes in Vara De Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from 1 review. More than 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vara De Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the overall difficulty level of mountain bike trails around Vara De Rey?
A: The majority of mountain bike trails in Vara De Rey are rated as moderate. Out of 18 available routes, 16 are moderate, offering a good balance for riders with some experience. There are also a couple of easier options for those looking for a less challenging ride.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly mountain bike trails in Vara De Rey?
A: Yes, while most trails are moderate, there are a few easier mountain bike routes available in the Vara De Rey area. These are suitable for beginners or those preferring a more relaxed ride through the agricultural plains and gentle hills.

Q: What are some of the longer mountain bike routes available?
A: For those seeking a longer ride, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017684194/las-torcas-de-sisante-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torcas de Sisante Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a notable option, covering approximately 55.5 km (34.5 miles) and typically taking around 4 hours 37 minutes to complete. Another substantial route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beautiful view of the Júcar River – Alarcón Castle loop from Tébar&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 43 km (26.7 miles) long.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that offer elevated views?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017691085/sierra-de-el-canavate-loop-from-vara-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de El Cañavate Loop from Vara de Rey&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for elevated views. This moderate 41.8 km (26.0 miles) route features ascents into the Sierra de El Cañavate, providing scenic panoramas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Vara De Rey?
A: Mountain biking in Vara De Rey offers varied terrain, primarily characterized by agricultural plains and gentle rolling hills. You'll also encounter areas with distinctive karst depressions and some more elevated sections, particularly around the Sierra de El Cañavate. The routes provide a mix of open paths and trails traversing rural settings.

Q: Are there any loop trails for mountain biking in Vara De Rey?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Vara De Rey are designed as loops. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017684194/las-torcas-de-sisante-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Torcas de Sisante Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017691085/sierra-de-el-canavate-loop-from-vara-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de El Cañavate Loop from Vara de Rey&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sisante loop from Sisante&lt;/a&gt;, all offering circular routes to explore the region.

Q: What are some interesting places to see near the mountain bike trails?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several interesting nearby attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of San Clemente&lt;/a&gt; or the historic town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5675996" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Clemente&lt;/a&gt; itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5319159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sisante Town Hall and Main Square&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into local life. For a cultural stop, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5769666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgen de Trascastillo (El Cañavate)&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point of interest.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Vara De Rey?
A: The mountain bike routes in Vara De Rey are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Over 60 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the mix of agricultural plains, gentle hills, and occasional karst features that define the area.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through rural landscapes and agricultural areas?
A: Yes, many routes in Vara De Rey traverse rural landscapes and agricultural areas, providing a peaceful and immersive experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20135648" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sisante loop from Sisante&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, leading you through these characteristic environments.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Vara De Rey?
A: The region of Vara De Rey, located in Castilla-La Mancha, generally experiences hot, dry summers and mild winters. The most comfortable times for mountain biking are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October), when temperatures are more moderate and the weather is generally pleasant for outdoor activities.

Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in the Vara De Rey area?
A: There are 18 mountain bike trails available for exploration around Vara De Rey, offering a range of options for different skill levels and preferences.
tips: Beautiful and large religious sanctuary located in the town of El Cañavate, from where you can enjoy aerial views of it. It is also known as the Virgin of Trascastillo.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Sierra de El Cañavate mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this mountain biking route is approximately 3 hours and 31 minutes. This is an average time and can vary based on your pace, fitness level, and how often you stop.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While it offers a good challenge, it's generally manageable for mountain bikers with some experience. The total elevation gain is around 367 meters over nearly 42 kilometers.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is a circular trail, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, offering a convenient loop experience without needing to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: Specific detailed information about the terrain for this particular mountain biking route is not readily available. However, given its location in the Sierra de El Cañavate, you can generally expect a mix of unpaved tracks, gravel paths, and potentially some singletrack sections typical of mountain biking in rural Spanish sierras. Be prepared for varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: One notable point of interest along the route is the Sanctuary of the Virgen de Trascastillo (El Cañavate). This religious building offers a cultural highlight during your ride.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 11% of this route follows sections of the Camino de Vara del Rey a Atalaya del Cañavate.

faqs.question: Is the Sierra de El Cañavate mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not available, generally, dogs are allowed on many trails in rural Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or regulations upon arrival to ensure compliance.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that specific permits or fees are required to access this mountain biking route. Most trails in this region are freely accessible. However, it's always good practice to respect private property and any local regulations you might encounter.

faqs.question: Is there parking available near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact trailhead are not detailed, Vara del Rey and Cañada Juncosa are small towns. You can typically find street parking in or near these villages, especially close to the start/end points of circular routes. Look for suitable, safe spots that do not obstruct local traffic or residents.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the climate in this part of Spain, spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times for mountain biking. Temperatures are milder, and the scenery is often pleasant. Summers can be very hot, making midday rides challenging, while winters can be cooler and potentially wet.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, this route might be challenging for absolute beginners or very young children. While specific terrain details are not available, the distance of nearly 42 kilometers and the elevation gain suggest it requires a reasonable level of fitness and mountain biking experience. Families with experienced teenage riders might find it enjoyable.
